codeReview.summary: The Zustand extraction and store split are directionally sound, but the feature did not actually satisfy its own App.jsx size target. That leaves the frontend architecture milestone failing a stated code-review gate before user testing starts.

issues.description: The refactor never got App.jsx under the required threshold. The feature contract and VAL-FRONTEND-002 both require `App.jsx < 400` lines, but `git show 74c1aef:src/App.jsx | wc -l` returns `400`, and the current frontend-refactor head is already back up to 417 lines. The feature therefore claimed success while still missing a validation gate the next milestone validator will check.
issues.suggestedFix: Move more derived-state and interaction logic into focused hooks or store selectors so App.jsx is strictly below 400 lines, then add a regression check that fails when the file reaches 400 lines or more.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The frontend-worker skill still suggests component rendering tests with Testing Library, but this repo has no DOM test stack. In practice the worker had to verify the Zustand refactor with store-level Node tests plus agent-browser checks.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: package.json only has Vite devDependencies and no vitest/jsdom/@testing-library packages, and handoff 2026-03-27T05-43-55-715Z__frontend-state-management__42756a81-b372-4b7e-a597-ee2cd57d48b2.json explicitly calls this out in skillFeedback.

summary: Fail. The state-store extraction is mostly well-structured, but it missed the explicit `App.jsx < 400` requirement that gates VAL-FRONTEND-002.
